sections.title: Muscle Basics
points: Definition: Muscles are elastic bundles of fibers that produce movement and maintain posture.
States: When a muscle contracts, it becomes shorter, thicker, and harder. When it relaxes, it becomes longer and thinner.
The Pull Rule: Muscles can ONLY PULL; they never push a bone back into position.

sections.title: Cardiac Muscle (The Specialist)
points: Found ONLY in the walls of the heart.
It combined features: It is striped (like skeletal) but automatic (like smooth).
It contracts rhythmically to pump blood and never fatigues throughout life.
It has a very rich blood supply to provide constant oxygen and glucose.

sections.title: What Muscles Need to Work
points: Fuel: Muscles need Glucose (sugar) and Oxygen to produce energy.
Waste: When muscles work too hard without enough oxygen, they produce Lactic Acid.
Fatigue: The buildup of lactic acid causes muscle cramp and tiredness.
Heat: Muscle contraction produces heat. This is why we shiver when cold to warm up the body.

sections.text: Exam Tip: The Diaphragm is a skeletal muscle that works automatically for breathing, but we can also control it when we want to hold our breath!

cards.fact: The 'striped' look of skeletal and cardiac muscles.
The waste product that causes muscle pain/fatigue.
Wave-like contraction of smooth muscles to push food.
